name: Guangdong–Hong Kong–Macau Greater Bay Area
climate: Subtropical monsoon; hot humid summers with typhoon season (Jun–Nov), mild winters; heavy rainfall

neighbors.name: Pearl River Delta
neighbors.relevance: GBA encompasses and extends the PRD manufacturing base; near-complete geographic overlap

neighbors.name: South China Sea
neighbors.relevance: GBA's ports open directly onto the South China Sea; shipping route for all exports

neighbors.name: Yangtze River Delta
neighbors.relevance: China's other major economic cluster; competes and complements GBA in finance and manufacturing

neighbors.name: ASEAN
neighbors.relevance: ASEAN is GBA's closest international trading partner bloc; factory relocation destination

neighbors.name: Taiwan Strait
neighbors.relevance: Semiconductor supply dependency on Taiwan; any disruption directly impacts GBA electronics industry

links.description: GBA subsumes the PRD and adds Hong Kong and Macau for financial and services integration
GBA ports (Shenzhen, Guangzhou, Hong Kong) are primary departure points for South China Sea shipping
Domestic rivalry and supply chain linkages between China's two largest economic clusters
ASEAN is the top trade partner and manufacturing relocation destination for GBA firms
GBA electronics and tech firms depend on TSMC and Taiwan semiconductor supply
Hong Kong and Shenzhen serve as financing and logistics hubs for Belt and Road projects
Energy imports and container exports transit the Strait of Malacca en route to GBA ports
